data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "The collapse was an endogenous political revolution where subject populations deliberately dismantled the palatial bureaucracy to escape tribute obligations during a period of climate-induced stress.", "mechanism": "Social contract rupture: localized revolts against extractive institutions became contagious across the network as the cost of rebellion dropped below the cost of submission.", "scope": {"covers": ["Mycenaean Greece", "Hittite Anatolia", "Ugarit", "Levantine city-states"], "excludes": ["Egypt"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Settlement patterns showing continuous occupation of palace sites by the same elite class immediately following the destruction horizon without cultural break.", "eval": "rubric:std-hist"}], "prose_notes": "An atypical, internalist explanation framing the event not as a catastrophe inflicted from outside or nature, but as a successful class struggle against the palace system itself."}
